reasoning: This animation sequence mimics the act of gently cradling a melon.  It starts with both arms outstretched, palms up, as if ready to receive the melon. Then, the arms move inward and slightly upward, cradling the imaginary melon. Finally, the arms settle into a comfortable, protective position, holding the melon close to the body.  The movement is slow and deliberate, emphasizing the care taken with the delicate fruit. The use of both arms creates a sense of balance and security in the gesture. The sequence will use a combination of joint1 and joint2 to create the gentle arc of the movement.  The wrists will remain mostly neutral, with slight adjustments to enhance the cradling motion.
